briefing.headline: Crank it — pre-spawn stained water calls for aggressive cranking on key structure.
briefing.gameplan:
In pre-spawn with stained water and pre frontal conditions, cranking presentations shine.

KVD: Fish the Red Eye Shad like a worm or jig, not just cast-and-reel. Rip it out of cover, let it flutter down — that swimming shimmy on the fall is the magic. The colder it is, the slower you fish. Downsize to 1/4oz on ultra-shallow flats (1-2ft). Upsize line to 17-20lb fluoro to slow the fall and

Key adjustment for pre frontal: aggressive, faster retrieves — fish are feeding actively before the front.

primaryApproach.retrieve: KVD: Fish the Red Eye Shad like a worm or jig, not just cast-and-reel. Rip it out of cover, let it flutter down — that swimming shimmy on the fall is the magic. The colder it is, the slower you fish.
primaryApproach.targets: Focus on flat, grass, creek channel. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

alternateApproach.retrieve: Wheeler: During spring the prime depth zone on most lakes is 6 feet or less; the Jabber Jaw Hybrid Squarebill dives 3-5 feet which is the right zone for most fisheries across the country.
alternateApproach.targets: Focus on flat, grass, creek channel. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

proInsights.insight: Wheeler: For a spring pre-spawn event limited to three rods, picks a jerkbait/crankbait for deeper staging fish, a 1/2-oz jig for versatility, and a ChatterBait for stained shallow water.

KVD: 'I pick up my fishing pace considerably as the water hits around 52°F. I may run down a bank quickly throwing a lipless crankbait or spinnerbait, then pause to pitch a jig at submerged logs or brushpiles.'

briefing.depthStrategy: Pre-frontal pre-spawn bass are aggressive and moving shallow. Expect fish in the 4-8ft range on secondary points and channel swings. Falling pressure pulls fish up from staging areas — target the first breakline off spawning flats.
adjustIf: If the front stalls and conditions stabilize, dial back aggression and focus on higher-percentage structure
If water clears significantly, downsize presentations and move to natural color patterns
If fish are short-striking, add a long pause after deflections to let the lure suspend
